title: mentha × villosa subvar. exserta
intro: Mentha × villosa subvar. exserta, commonly known as Exserted Woolly Mint, is a subspecies of the Mentha villosa species, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Exserted Woolly Mint include its ability to alleviate nausea, improve digestion, and reduce inflammation, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Exserted Woolly Mint can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or used topically for its medicinal benefits.
